steps: Go to https://stytch.com/dashboard and log in or create a free Stytch account
Create a new project by clicking your project name in the top-left corner and selecting 'Create Project'
Choose 'Consumer Authentication' or 'B2B Authentication' depending on your use case
Navigate to the 'API Keys' tab in the left sidebar (or go directly to https://stytch.com/dashboard/api-keys)
Copy your 'Project ID' (acts as the username) and 'Secret' (acts as the password)
Stytch uses HTTP Basic Authentication: encode 'project_id:secret' in Base64 and pass as the Authorization header

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Encoded Basic Auth Credential
credential_fields.description: Base64-encoded string of 'project_id:secret'. In the Authorization header this is sent as 'Basic <encoded_value>'.

notes: Free tier available (up to 25 monthly active users for test environments)
Stytch uses HTTP Basic Auth: Project ID is the username, Secret is the password
Each project has a Test environment (test.stytch.com) and a Live environment (api.stytch.com)
Use the Test environment (test.stytch.com) for development — credentials are separate per environment
Secret is displayed in the dashboard and can be regenerated if compromised

test_endpoint.description: Search for users (empty query returns empty list) to verify Basic Auth credentials
